title: Add pointer influence to scene motion

tests.title: pointerDisturbance decays gradually after pointer becomes idle
tests.description: When pointer is active on frame 1 then becomes idle on subsequent frames, pointerDisturbance should remain non-zero on the next frame (not drop instantly to 0) due to EMA smoothing in the render loop.
tests.section: US-013: Pointer disturbance smoothing in render loop
tests.approach: Use the same RAF-mocking pattern as T-019-01 (treble smoothing). Provide a getPointerState that returns active+moving on frame 1, then idle (speed=0, active=false) on frames 2+. Capture params.pointerDisturbance from the geometry draw spy across frames. Assert frame 2's pointerDisturbance is still > 0 (decay, not instant drop).
assertions: Frame 2 pointerDisturbance > 0 (smoothed decay, not instant zero)
acceptance_criteria: Effect decays when pointer becomes idle
dependencies: Step 1: EMA smoothing for pointer disturbance

mocks: getPointerState — return moving pointer on frame 1, idle on frames 2+

tests.title: pointerDisturbance approaches zero after sustained idle period
tests.description: After pointer becomes idle, running many frames should bring pointerDisturbance very close to zero, confirming the decay converges.
tests.section: US-013: Pointer disturbance smoothing in render loop
tests.approach: Same setup as T-013-01 but run 60+ frames after pointer goes idle. Assert the final frame's pointerDisturbance is < 0.01 (near zero).
acceptance_criteria: Effect decays when pointer becomes idle
dependencies: Step 1: EMA smoothing for pointer disturbance

tests.title: pointerDisturbance ramps up smoothly on pointer movement start
tests.description: When pointer starts moving after being idle, pointerDisturbance should increase gradually (not jump to max), confirming smooth ramp-up via EMA.
tests.section: US-013: Pointer disturbance smoothing in render loop
tests.approach: Start with idle pointer for 2 frames, then switch to active high-speed pointer. The first active frame's pointerDisturbance should be less than subsequent active frames (gradual ramp). Assert monotonic increase across the first few active frames.
assertions: First active frame pointerDisturbance < second active frame pointerDisturbance (ramping up)
acceptance_criteria: Pointer changes at least one motion parameter
dependencies: Step 1: EMA smoothing for pointer disturbance

mocks: getPointerState — idle frames 1-2, then active with speed=0.1 on frames 3+

tests.title: particles near pointer position displace more than distant ones
tests.description: When pointerDisturbance > 0 and pointer position is provided, particles close to the pointer should be displaced more than particles far from it, creating a spatial 'wake' effect.
tests.approach: Initialize two identical particle fields with same seed. Run one frame with pointerDisturbance=0.8 and pointerX/pointerY set to a known position. After drawing, compare displacement of particles that were initially near the pointer vs far from it. Near particles should have moved more.
assertions: Average displacement of particles within 0.2 of pointer > average displacement of particles beyond 0.5 from pointer
acceptance_criteria: Pointer changes at least one motion parameter

setup.notes: Use FrameState with pointerX/pointerY fields. Compare against a control run with pointerDisturbance=0.

tests.title: zero pointerDisturbance produces no extra particle displacement
tests.description: When pointerDisturbance is 0, particles should move identically to a run without any pointer influence — confirming pointer code is properly gated.
tests.approach: Run two identical particle fields with same seed and same params (pointerDisturbance=0), one with pointerX/Y set and one without. Positions after draw should be identical.
assertions: Positions match exactly when pointerDisturbance=0 regardless of pointer position values

setup.notes: This is a regression/guard test ensuring pointer position alone doesn't cause unwanted displacement.

tests.title: idle scene without pointer input still animates (intentional motion)
tests.description: With pointerDisturbance=0, bassEnergy=0, trebleEnergy=0, particles should still move due to base cadence/motionAmplitude — confirming idle mode looks intentional.
tests.approach: Initialize particle field, record positions, run several frames with all energy params at 0, confirm positions have changed due to base velocity.
assertions: Particle positions after 10 frames differ from initial positions

setup.notes: Similar to T-009-12 but explicitly with all reactive params zeroed.

tests.title: pointer position is threaded through FrameState to geometry system
tests.description: When getPointerState returns a position, the FrameState passed to geometry.draw should include pointerX and pointerY fields matching the normalized pointer position.
tests.section: US-013: Pointer disturbance smoothing in render loop
tests.approach: Provide getPointerState returning {x: 0.3, y: 0.7, ...}. Capture FrameState from geometry draw spy. Assert frameState.pointerX === 0.3 and frameState.pointerY === 0.7.
acceptance_criteria: Pointer changes at least one motion parameter
dependencies: Step 3: Thread pointer position through FrameState

tests.title: smoothed pointerDisturbance stays within 0-1 range across all conditions
tests.description: Regardless of input pointer speed, the smoothed pointerDisturbance value passed to geometry should always be in [0, 1].
tests.section: US-013: Pointer disturbance smoothing in render loop
tests.approach: Run frames with extreme pointer speeds (speed=100), alternating active/idle. Assert all pointerDisturbance values are >= 0 and <= 1. Pattern after T-019-02.
acceptance_criteria: Pointer changes at least one motion parameter

tests.title: particle positions stay within bounds with maximum pointer disturbance
tests.description: Running many frames with pointerDisturbance=1 should not cause particles to escape [0, 1] bounds.
tests.approach: Run 100 frames with pointerDisturbance=1 and pointerX/Y set. Assert all particle positions remain in [0, 1]. Pattern after T-019-05.
acceptance_criteria: Pointer changes at least one motion parameter

tests.title: touch and mouse both produce non-zero pointerDisturbance in render loop
tests.description: Both touch-originated and mouse-originated pointer states should produce equivalent pointerDisturbance values, confirming both input types influence the scene.
tests.section: US-013: Pointer disturbance smoothing in render loop
tests.approach: Run two separate loop instances: one with a pointer state simulating mouse (active=true, speed=0.05) and one simulating touch (same values — pointer.ts normalizes both). Assert both produce the same non-zero pointerDisturbance. This is a contract test since pointer.ts already unifies touch/mouse, but it validates the integration.
assertions: Touch-like pointer state with same speed produces same pointerDisturbance
acceptance_criteria: Touch and mouse both influence the scene

setup.notes: Since pointer.ts abstracts touch/mouse into the same PointerState, identical speed values produce identical disturbance. This test validates the contract at the render loop level.

test_files.new_section: US-013: Pointer disturbance smoothing in render loop
test_files.placement: After the US-012 describe block, before the privacy describe block
After the US-011 describe block, before the privacy describe block
